data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G4 (child consistency) — parent epic `5fd2` × child `db7b` (REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored sidecar). 7-mode cross-child interaction sweep against the WHOLE sibling roster; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied. ONE finding (modes: CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS + SCOPE OVERLAP — "override justifications"), then the affirmed-clean modes.

═══ FINDING — Mode 2/4 (conflicting assumption + scope overlap): db7b's "What" says the sidecar captures "override justifications," but override logging is OWNED by sibling `092b` as a claim-path comment, NOT by the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar — and the shipped sidecar payload captures no such field ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `db7b` §What ("A reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + **override justifications**") vs sibling `092b` §What/AC2 ("--force + justification override (recorded)"; "--force override is available and logged with justification") + parent `5fd2` §Observability/FP-substrate AC ("`REVIEW_RESULT` reducer-ignored sidecar event with finding fingerprints; reducer skips the type; **override logging**; offline-analysis-ready").
- evidence:
  - db7b's description asserts the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar payload itself carries "override justifications." But the OWNER of the `--force` override audit trail is sibling `092b`: its completion-verifier PASS confirms `_plan_review_precheck` writes a `FORCE_CLAIM: …` COMMENT (head-state, via `leaf.comment`) with the justification on the CLAIM PATH — a different artifact (a normal comment event) on a different code path (the fast claim-path precheck), NOT the out-of-band review's REVIEW_RESULT sidecar.
  - Grounded in code: the implemented sidecar payload (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:85-130` `build_payload`/`_slim`) captures `schema/verdict/ticket_id/ticket_type/material_fingerprint/model/runner/metrics/coverage/findings/coaching` — and NO override/justification field whatsoever. `search_files "override"` over `sidecar.py` returns ZERO matches. So the sidecar as shipped does NOT capture override justifications; the db7b §What clause is unfulfilled by db7b's own implementation, and the datum actually lives in `092b`'s claim-path comment.
  - This is precisely the kind of cross-child contract ambiguity G4 guards against: the parent's sidecar AC names "override logging" in the same bullet as the sidecar, db7b's §What pulls "override justifications" INTO the sidecar, but `092b` (the claim/override owner) records them as a claim-path comment instead. The two children's specs disagree on WHICH artifact holds the override-justification record. The shipped code resolves it `092b`'s way (claim-path comment); db7b's §What prose was never reconciled.
  - Note this divergence was actually MIS-AFFIRMED by the G3 review of this same pairing (in db7b's comments), which read db7b's §What "override justifications" clause as making override-logging co-owned and therefore "not orphaned." That G3 affirmation is consistent with db7b's PROSE but inconsistent with the SHIPPED artifact split — corroborating that the spec text is genuinely ambiguous about ownership.
- scenarios:
  - An analyst building the offline FP/remediation join reads db7b §What, expects the override justification on each REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (to correlate `--force` bypasses with the review verdict), and finds no such field — the override record is on a separate `FORCE_CLAIM:` comment keyed only by ticket_id/time, on the claim path, with no finding-id linkage to the review it bypassed.
  - A future reviser, taking db7b §What literally, adds an override field to the sidecar payload — duplicating the record `092b` already owns on the claim path (double-write of the same datum across two children / two artifacts).
- impact: A parent↔child / child↔child consistency defect (not a coverage gap — G3 confirms the parent's "override logging" lane is covered, just by `092b` not by db7b). Low blast-radius (shipped behavior is coherent: `092b` owns the comment), but db7b's spec text claims a sidecar capability the sidecar does not implement and that a sibling already owns elsewhere, which can mislead the offline-analysis consumer and invite a duplicate implementation.
- checklist_item:
  - [ ] Reconcile db7b §What with the shipped artifact split: either (a) strike "+ override justifications" from db7b's REVIEW_RESULT capture list and state that the `--force` override justification is owned by `092b` as a claim-path `FORCE_CLAIM:` comment (head-state), referenced for offline analysis by ticket_id (the parent's "override logging" is then covered by `092b`, not the sidecar); OR (b) if the sidecar genuinely should carry it, add an explicit `override`/`force_justification` field to `build_payload`/`_slim` (`sidecar.py:85-130`) AND define how it is keyed to the review/finding, and de-duplicate against `092b`'s claim-path comment so the datum has one owner.
- suggested_fix: Adopt (a) — remove "override justifications" from db7b's sidecar §What and add: "the `--force` override justification is recorded by `092b` on the claim path (a `FORCE_CLAIM:` head-state comment), joined offline by ticket_id; the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar carries per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metrics + coverage only." This matches the shipped `sidecar.py` payload and the `092b` ownership, removing the cross-child ambiguity without changing behavior.

═══ AFFIRMED-CLEAN MODES (the other interaction modes) ═══
1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — clean. The load-bearing shared datum is the per-finding `id`/content-fingerprint: SINGLE owner = orchestrator `ca03` (`mint_finding_id`, SOLE owner), CONSUMED by `75a9` (coach `finding_refs`) and RECORDED by `db7b` (sidecar). Parent, `ca03`, `75a9`, and `db7b` all state this identically — non-circular, no double-write (db7b RECORDS, never mints). The REVIEW_RESULT event TYPE is also shared with the reducer/compaction/fsck/rollout surface (`_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`), but db7b is the sole owner of the sidecar event and the type-registration is coordinated (verify-PASS), not implicit.
2. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — only the Finding above. The latency/cost AC (db7b: "the fast claim-path check is structurally LLM/network-free, recorded as a constant marker") is CONSISTENT with `092b`'s structural "No LLM/network on the claim path" AC and the parent's explicit split (the 50ms STRUCTURAL proving check → `092b`; the per-pass latency/cost CAPTURE → db7b) — complementary, no contradiction. The corrected Scope note (retention is a dedicated `sidecar.prune`, NOT compaction, "because REVIEW_RESULT is reducer-IGNORED, compaction PRESERVES it") resolved the earlier compaction self-contradiction the gate flagged on this very ticket — now internally consistent with the reducer-ignored design.
3. DEPENDENCY GAP — clean (benign-reading). db7b records the `id` minted by `ca03` and referenced by `75a9`; db7b carries `relates_to 75a9` and the parent/`ca03`/`75a9` prose pins the mint→record→reference contract. The absence of an explicit `relates_to ca03` link is link-hygiene only (db7b is a passive recorder, contract fully specified in prose) — not a high-confidence sequencing/dependency gap.
4. SCOPE OVERLAP — clean beyond the Finding. db7b's "ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence" AC (preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones, fsck WARNs on newer types, readers-before-writers) is db7b's LEGITIMATE lane: superseded sibling `ce75`'s own G3/G4 reviews explicitly name `db7b` as the owner of the sidecar+signature rollout-ordering, and `092b` owns only the gate-off-preference enforcement on the claim path — no live double-ownership. db7b's slice (capture/observability substrate + prune + rollout-coexistence for the new events) does not overlap any active sibling.
5. ORDERING VIOLATION — clean. db7b is a terminal RECORDER of the post-Pass-3 assembled result; it does not feed back into the block/advisory decision and is correctly part of the fan-out after the proven MVP spine (012e/ca03/1913/acc1/487d/4bb7/092b). The prune runs best-effort AFTER emit, off the hot paths — no ordering hazard.
6. CONSUMER IMPACT — clean. db7b introduces a NEW reducer-IGNORED event type; older clones preserve-and-ignore it (verify-PASS; `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` + `is_unknown_newer_type`), head/.cache/deps/validate unaffected (AC2). No existing consumer broken; the append-only-per-run vs idempotency-on-attestation split is internally consistent with `4bb7`/`092b`.
7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — clean. Every artifact db7b references resolves live: `ca03` (id-minter, CLOSED), `75a9` (coach, CLOSED), and the in-repo reducer/compaction/fsck machinery (`_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`, `compact.py`, `fsck.py`). The earlier "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" residual mis-reference was corrected in-Scope to the dedicated `sidecar.prune` — no dangling/removed reference remains.

NET: ONE G4 finding — child `db7b` §What ("override justifications" captured by the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar) conflicts with sibling `092b`'s ownership of override logging (a claim-path `FORCE_CLAIM:` comment) and with db7b's own shipped sidecar payload (`sidecar.py:85-130` records no override field). The shipped behavior is coherent (`092b` owns the comment), so this is a spec-text/ownership reconciliation, not a behavioral defect. The other 6 interaction modes are clean and consistent with the parent and every interacting sibling (`ca03`/`75a9`/`092b`/`4bb7`/`ce75`).
